sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Group Shared Services Centre (Finance / HR / IT / Procurement)
condition_precedents: Group-level SSC mandate approved by Group CEO / Board
Service charter, SLA framework and chargeback model agreed with all BUs
HR / IR clearance for role transitions and union consultation completed
industry_sources: Deloitte — Global Shared Services & Outsourcing Survey 2024 (deloitte.com/content/dam/Deloitte/global/Documents/Tax/dttl-tax-global-shared-services-survey.pdf) — 25-50% cost reduction benchmarks for SSC migration
McKinsey & Company — Transforming the Back Office (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights) — shared services ROI studies across large conglomerates
Hackett Group — World-Class Shared Services Performance 2024 (thehackettgroup.com/research/) — G&A cost reduction through SSC

execution_playbook.step: Assess process maturity and standardisation readiness across F&A, HR, and IT sub-processes; identify SSC candidate scope by BU
Design SSC operating model: location, governance charter, SLA framework, and chargeback model
Execute migration in waves—Wave 1 transactional F&A (AP/AR/GL), Wave 2 HR operations, Wave 3 IT service desk
Stabilise SSC with SLA monitoring dashboard; enforce chargeback to business units to drive demand discipline

diagnostic_signals.signal: Duplicate F&A or HR back-office teams across >3 business units performing identical transactional processes
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Consolidation will eliminate redundancy and reduce per-transaction cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Cost per transaction (e.g., cost per invoice processed) >2× SSC benchmark for same process
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Scale economies from SSC are material

diagnostic_signals.signal: Multiple ERP instances or process variants across BUs for the same transactional process
diagnostic_signals.confirms: SSC standardisation will reduce technology and training cost

required_data_fields: F&A, HR, and IT process inventory with volume, FTE, and cost per BU
Current cost-per-transaction benchmarks by process
ERP/technology landscape across business units
Chargeback/recharge model and inter-company billing data
Location analysis for SSC hub (cost, talent, infrastructure)

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Group Procurement Aggregation & Preferred Supplier Panels
condition_precedents: Spend aggregation across BUs completed for common categories (IT, travel, MRO, facilities)
Group preferred supplier panel approved with volume rebate structure
BU compliance to preferred supplier mandated via policy (with derogation process)
industry_sources: Hackett Group — World-Class Procurement Performance Study 2024 (thehackettgroup.com/research/) — group procurement aggregation savings 8-20%
CAPS Research — Group Purchasing & Consortium Benchmarks (capsresearch.org/benchmarking/) — spend aggregation leverage benchmarks
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Aggregate spend data across all group entities; identify categories with >₹10 Cr combined spend and multiple BU buyers
Establish cross-BU category councils for top-5 common categories; align specifications and demand volumes
Run group-level RFPs and negotiate master vendor agreements; commit total volume in exchange for tiered pricing
Mandate compliance to group contracts via P2P system controls; track savings by BU monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Same vendor supplying multiple group BUs at different unit prices—price spread >15%
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Group aggregation will capture price harmonisation savings

diagnostic_signals.signal: Category spend above market benchmark across ≥3 BUs with no group-level contract in place
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Group RFP will yield material savings

diagnostic_signals.signal: Tail vendor count >500 across group for categories with <10 suitable suppliers in market
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Supplier rationalisation will reduce admin and negotiating leverage fragmentation

required_data_fields: Consolidated spend data across all group entities by category and vendor
Vendor master with prices by BU for common categories
Existing contracts by BU with term and pricing details
Demand forecast by category for next 12 months across group
Specification alignment assessment for common categories

condition_precedents: Span-and-layer analysis completed for corporate centre
Role redesign and delayering plan approved by CHRO and Group CEO
Remaining corporate roles clearly delineated from BU roles to prevent drift
industry_sources: McKinsey & Company — Why Zero-Based Budgeting Makes Sense Again (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/why-zero-based-budgeting-makes-sense-again) — 10-25% corporate overhead reduction
CII — India Inc. Cost Competitiveness Report 2024 (cii.in) — corporate overhead benchmarks for Indian conglomerates
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Build ZBB baseline for all corporate overhead cost lines; map every FTE and spend item to business-justification owner
Benchmark corporate overhead as % of group revenue against listed-peer conglomerates; identify gap to top-quartile
Define elimination and reduction targets by function; model span-of-control and layer count vs benchmark
Execute reduction plan with HR/legal review; enforce new span and layer guardrails in org design
Track overhead cost as % of revenue quarterly; gate new corporate hiring against overhead budget

diagnostic_signals.signal: Corporate overhead as % of group revenue >3% vs peer conglomerate median of <2%
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Structural overhead reduction opportunity is material

diagnostic_signals.signal: Corporate headcount growing faster than group revenue over past 3 years
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Overhead creep requires reset

diagnostic_signals.signal: Management layers at group HQ >6 from CEO to first-line manager
diagnostic_signals.confirms: De-layering opportunity exists

required_data_fields: Full corporate overhead P&L by function and cost line
Headcount by function and layer at group HQ
Peer benchmark: corporate overhead % of revenue for listed conglomerates
Group revenue and EBITDA trend (3 years)
Org chart with span-of-control data by function

condition_precedents: Portfolio strategy review completed (core / adjacent / non-core classification)
Non-core business units identified with divestiture rationale approved by Board
Investment banker / M&A adviser engaged for carve-out or sale process
industry_sources: McKinsey & Company — The Five Traps of Portfolio Rationalisation (mckinsey.com/capabilities/strategy-and-corporate-finance) — divestiture impact on EBITDA margins
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Score all group businesses on two dimensions: strategic fit (core vs adjacency) and financial performance (ROCE vs WACC)
Develop divestiture/exit business cases for bottom-quartile businesses; size value at stake vs holding cost
Engage advisors and run structured M&A/divestiture process for targeted exits; manage data room and buyer due diligence
Redeploy capital from exits to high-ROCE businesses; track holding cost elimination and capital reallocation

diagnostic_signals.signal: ≥2 portfolio businesses with ROCE below WACC for ≥3 consecutive years
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Value destruction is ongoing and exit/restructure is economically justified

diagnostic_signals.signal: Corporate holding cost (HQ allocation + intercompany service charges) >5% of revenue for sub-scale businesses
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Holding cost drag compounds the underperformance

diagnostic_signals.signal: Capital tied in non-core businesses restricting investment in strategic growth engines
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Portfolio rationalisation will free capital for redeployment

required_data_fields: Business-unit P&L, ROCE, and capital employed (3-year history)
Strategic classification of each business (core / adjacency / non-core)
WACC by business unit and group level
Holding cost allocation (HQ charges, intercompany services) by business
M&A market conditions and indicative valuation for exit candidates

condition_precedents: Group insurance inventory completed across all BUs and geographies
Risk aggregation analysis confirming captive viability (premium > ₹50 Cr typical)
Insurance broker with group programme expertise engaged
industry_sources: Aon — Global Insurance Market Conditions Report 2024 (aon.com/risk-services/risk-consulting/insurance-market-update.jsp) — group pooling discount benchmarks 10-25%
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Consolidate insurance inventory across all group entities: policy type, sum insured, premium, insurer, and renewal date
Design group master policy (Property All Risk, Group Health, D&O, Marine) with combined limits and aggregation clauses
Run group tender (broker RFP); negotiate combined premium with top-3 insurers using full group exposure data
Consolidate all entities onto group master policy at renewal; track premium savings and claims ratio annually

diagnostic_signals.signal: Same risk class (e.g., property, group health) insured by different insurers across ≥3 group entities at different rates
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Pooling and combined negotiation will capture volume discount

diagnostic_signals.signal: Average group health or property insurance premium >10% above market benchmark for same risk profile
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Market comparison confirms over-payment

diagnostic_signals.signal: No group-level insurance broker or procurement structure in place
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Centralised procurement will immediately improve negotiating position

required_data_fields: Insurance policy inventory across all group entities (type, sum insured, premium, insurer, renewal)
Claims history by entity and policy type (3 years)
Broker arrangement and brokerage fee disclosure
Benchmark premium rates for equivalent risk profiles from market survey
Group risk register for key insured assets and liabilities

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Intercompany Transfer Pricing & Shared Cost Optimisation
condition_precedents: Transfer pricing documentation updated and benchmarked to arm's-length standards
Shared cost allocation methodology reviewed and aligned with OECD / Indian TP rules
Tax adviser sign-off on revised intercompany pricing structure obtained
industry_sources: OECD — Transfer Pricing Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises 2022 (oecd.org/tax/transfer-pricing/) — arm's length principle for intercompany pricing
PwC — International Transfer Pricing 2024 (pwc.com/gx/en/services/tax/international-tax/transfer-pricing.html) — transfer pricing optimisation frameworks
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Map all intercompany transactions by BU pair: type (services, goods, IP royalty), volume, and current transfer price
Benchmark each transaction category against arm's-length range using comparable uncontrolled pricing or TNMM method
Redesign TP policy to comply with Ind AS 115 and OECD BEPS Action 13; update intercompany agreements
Update ERP intercompany billing configuration to enforce new TP policy; file Form 3CEB and TP documentation

diagnostic_signals.signal: Intercompany service charges as % of BU revenue >8% for non-core shared services
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Repricing or scope reduction will reduce cost at receiving BU

diagnostic_signals.signal: Transfer prices not reviewed for >2 years despite changes in group structure or market rates
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Benchmarking gap creates TP risk and potential over/under-charging

diagnostic_signals.signal: Tax authority transfer-pricing adjustment or notice in past 3 years
diagnostic_signals.confirms: TP policy documentation and defence gap is material

required_data_fields: Intercompany transaction register by BU pair (type, volume, price, currency)
Existing intercompany service agreements and royalty agreements
Comparable market benchmarks for each service/IP category
Group structure chart with ownership percentages and jurisdictions
Prior-year Form 3CEB and TP documentation

condition_precedents: Group RE inventory mapped with utilisation, lease terms and market value
Sale-leaseback opportunities identified and appraised
Hybrid / remote work policy formalised to validate consolidation quantum
industry_sources: JLL India — India Office Market Report 2024 (jll.com/en-in/research/india-office-market-report) — office space rationalisation benchmarks
CBRE — India Real Estate Market Outlook 2024 (cbre.com/insights/reports/india-real-estate-market-outlook) — real estate portfolio optimisation
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Build group real estate inventory: location, area (sq ft), occupancy, lease terms, WALE, and annual rent/depreciation
Run space-utilisation sensing (badge/Wi-Fi data or manual survey) to measure actual vs designed occupancy
Prioritise lease exits, subleases, and format changes for under-utilised spaces; target WALE reduction in high-cost locations
Execute exits, consolidations, and renegotiations at lease-break or expiry events; implement hybrid-work policy to reduce seat demand
Track cost-per-seat, utilisation rate, and occupancy cost as % of revenue quarterly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Office occupancy rate <60% of designed capacity for ≥6 months post-pandemic baseline
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Consolidation and sublease opportunity is material

diagnostic_signals.signal: Occupancy cost (rent + depreciation) as % of group revenue >2% vs peer benchmark <1.5%
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Real estate cost reduction will materially improve margin

diagnostic_signals.signal: Lease WALE >4 years in locations where headcount has declined >20% over 3 years
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Lease-break or sublease strategy will reduce long-term commitment

required_data_fields: Group real estate inventory (location, area, occupancy rate, lease terms, WALE, annual cost)
Space utilisation data by floor/building (badge, Wi-Fi, or survey-based)
Headcount trend by location (3 years)
Lease contracts with break-clause and expiry dates
Sublease market rate benchmarks for key locations

condition_precedents: Legal and regulatory feasibility of cash pooling confirmed per entity jurisdiction
In-house bank structure designed and approved by board
ERP / treasury management system configured for netting transactions
industry_sources: Atradius — Payment Practices Barometer Asia-Pacific 2024 (atradius.us/knowledge-and-research/reports/payment-practices-barometer-asia-pacific.html) — working capital benchmarks
Hackett Group — Working Capital Management 2024 (thehackettgroup.com/working-capital-management-hackett/) — cash pooling and netting benefits
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Map all intra-group cash flows by currency, BU pair, and frequency; calculate gross vs net settlement requirements
Design multi-currency netting centre structure; select netting platform (bank-hosted or in-house); confirm FEMA and RBI compliance
Rationalise banking panel—retain top-tier banks for netting hub; close low-volume subsidiary accounts
Go live on netting platform; automate settlement runs (weekly/monthly); track bank fee and FX cost reduction

diagnostic_signals.signal: Gross intra-group cross-currency settlements >10× net exposure, indicating large gross-to-net compression opportunity
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Netting will materially reduce FX conversion and bank fees

diagnostic_signals.signal: Number of group bank accounts >50 across entities in same country with separate settlement cycles
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Account rationalisation will reduce bank fees and treasury overhead

diagnostic_signals.signal: Bank transaction fees for intra-group transfers >₹1 Cr/year
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Netting will eliminate most of these fees

required_data_fields: Intra-group cash flow register by BU pair, currency, and frequency (12 months)
Group bank account register with balances, fees, and transaction volumes
FEMA and RBI compliance checklist for netting/pooling structures
FX conversion costs by currency pair and BU
Netting platform vendor options and integration requirements

source_verification_note: industry_sources entries indicate the published works that directionally support each lever's savings thesis. Where no external benchmark was found, INTERNAL_CALIBRATION is noted. All cited sources are real publications; specific savings percentages remain internal calibrations unless the source directly cites a matching range.
